task: Write a Python function truncate_words(s, n) returning the first n whitespace-separated words of the string joined by single spaces, so runs of whitespace collapse. When n is zero or negative return ''. When the string has fewer than n words return them all. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function meets all the criteria with high scores, but could be improved in terms of naming an edge case.
trace: I used the `split()` method to separate the input string into a list of words, and then checked if there are fewer words than the requested number. If so, I joined all the found words together with a single space. Otherwise, I sliced the list of words up to the nth element and joined them together. The edge case handled is when the input string has less than 'n' words.
